Toronto · Summer 2027

Art made
by everyone.
Held by one thread.

A city-wide participatory art project where every Torontonian contributes something small to a growing collective work — and receives something in return.

20 Host Locations· 5 Neighbourhoods· 100% Recycled Glass· Jan 2027 — Jan 2028· DesignTO Exhibition· Community Impact
What is this

A living installation
built by the crowd

Guests arrive, make a bead string at a making station, and hang it on a collective structure. The string is exchanged for food and drink vouchers. The installation grows denser throughout the day — a physical record of everyone who took part.

01
Make
String your own beads at an artist-guided making station outside the installation.
02
Contribute
Hang your string on the curtain frame. Watch it become part of the growing collective work.
03
Receive
Exchange your string for one food voucher and one drink voucher from our sponsors.
04
Explore
Move through artist-led activations, eat, drink, and come back to contribute again.
The installation at dusk
The frame at setup
Recycled glass hourglass
Making stations
Aerial view
The hourglass

A city-wide
time capsule

Cast from recycled Toronto restaurant bottles and filled slowly with public bead donations — a living countdown placed across the city beginning January 2027.

About the object
Mold-cast from reclaimed beer & wine bottles
Each hourglass is cast from green and amber glass collected at participating Toronto restaurants. No wooden frame, no metal rods — just a wide-neck mold-cast form with removable flat glass lids, made to hold beads contributed by the public over six months.
20
Host Locations
5
Neighbourhoods
6
Months Active
100%
Recycled Glass
Summer 2027 · Toronto
Every string counts.
Every person leaves
a mark.
The project

Four phases,
one common thread

From collecting glass off Toronto restaurant floors to a winter exhibition at DesignTO — the project unfolds across 18 months.

Aug – Dec 2026
Preparing Materials
  • Mold made (Aug)
  • Glass collected (Sep)
  • Glass breaking (Oct)
  • Melting & forming (Nov–Dec)
Jan – Jun 2027
Pre-Event Time Capsule
  • Hourglass placing (Jan)
  • Public bead collection
  • Host locations active
  • Sponsor cross-promo
Jul – Oct 2027
During Event
Choose one
Option A
Summer Standalone
  • Outdoor live event
  • Artist activations
  • Social spaces
Option B
Nuit Blanche
  • Nuit Blanche activation
  • Night installation
  • Artist activations
January 2028
Post-Event
  • DesignTO festival
  • Silent auction
  • Community celebration
  • Charity donations

Partner types

How partners
get involved

Four ways to participate — each with a clear exchange of value for the project and for your brand.

Materials
Gives
  • Recyclable glass (wine & beer bottles) for hourglass time capsules
Receives
  • Interactive map placement
  • Brand exposure from hourglass host locations for 6 months
Host Location
Gives
  • Keeps hourglass in space for 6 months
  • Splits fixed cost of mold creation
Receives
  • Interactive map placement, 6 months cross-promotional exposure
  • During-event brand acknowledgement
  • Opportunity to keep or donate hourglass
Pledged Donation
Gives
  • Pledges $ amount to charity per string created
Receives
  • Website placement, 6 months pre-event brand exposure
  • During-event acknowledgement
  • Post-event brand promotion
Vendors
Gives
  • 1 food or drink item per string made during the event
Receives
  • Interactive map placement, 6 months exposure
  • Onsite brand presence
  • Revenue opportunity via event-day sales

Get involved

Become a contributor

Common Thread is built by a community of artists, venues, and supporters. Select how you'd like to contribute.

Apply as an artist
Lead a participatory activation at the event. Open to artists working in any medium — especially beadwork, craft, drawing, and material-based practices.
Food sponsor
Provide food at the event in exchange for branding, voucher traffic, and direct access to a highly engaged creative audience.
Drink sponsor
Supply beverages at the event. Every participant receives a drink voucher — your brand is embedded in the core participation loop.
Pledge support
Support the artist program, production costs, or the DesignTO winter exhibition. Donation sponsors are credited across all event materials.
Your name

Design TO · January 2028

The winter exhibition

All collective works — bead curtain sections, artist-led pieces, and the recycled glass hourglasses — are exhibited and auctioned at a DesignTO winter exhibition.

The exhibition

DesignTO · January 2028

Summer Event · 2027
Collective works are made by hundreds of participants over a single day in an outdoor park setting.
Winter Exhibition · Jan 2028
Works exhibited at a DesignTO venue. Silent auction with proceeds to artists and community causes.
Ongoing Legacy
Bead curtain sections installed in participating restaurants as permanent art, connected to the host network.
